vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

requirements.role: Non-weapon camera/gimbal sensor pod bay with removable insert and window

requirements.description: Payload and structural loads transfer from spar to payload bay
Optical path from window to camera sensor
Thermal dissipation from camera to spar and wing structure
Service access for camera and gimbal maintenance

risk.desc: Potential structural weakness at the spar reinforcement due to thin material.
risk.mit: Increase material thickness or use additional reinforcement.

risk.desc: Cooling inlet may not provide sufficient airflow for motor pod.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the cooling inlet or add additional cooling paths.

risk.desc: Bay door hinge may not be robust enough for long-term use.
risk.mit: Use a stronger material for the hinge or redesign the hinge mechanism.

generated_parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
generated_parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

requirements: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path
Transfers lift and landing loads through spar to fuselage frames
Heat from sun is transferred to cooling airflow over wing surface
Wingtip flexion and torsion are transferred through spar to wing root
Battery removal access through wing root service cutout
Electrical harness routing from avionics bay to wing tip sensors

verify.value: All required features are present and named correctly.
verify.rule: All required evidence_features must appear in part_tree/geometry_ops
cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value

verify.value: All features are traceable by id/name.
verify.rule: part_tree children and geometry_ops must be mutually traceable by id/name

verify.value: All interfaces have reciprocal naming and joint family declaration.
verify.rule: name exact neighboring subsystem labels and choose connection_type + DOF + clearance_mm + required_feature

verify.value: Visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on main spar due to direct sun exposure.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management strategies such as heat sinks or reflective coatings.

risk.desc: Risk of torsional twisting if anti-rotation key is not properly aligned.
risk.mit: Ensure precise alignment and locking of anti-rotation key during assembly.

risk.desc: Battery removal access might be compromised if service cutout is obstructed.
risk.mit: Ensure clear and unobstructed access to service cutout for battery removal.

risk.desc: Electrical harness routing could be compromised if not properly managed.
risk.mit: Ensure proper routing and protection of electrical harnesses.

risk.desc: Structural integrity may be compromised if rib attachment points fail.
risk.mit: Conduct thorough testing of rib attachment points under simulated load conditions.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path

physics_paths.description: Transfers lift and landing loads through spar to fuselage frames
Heat from sun is transferred to cooling airflow over wing surface
Wingtip flexion and torsion are transferred through spar to wing root
Battery removal access through wing root service cutout
Electrical harness routing from avionics bay to wing tip sensors

integration_opportunities.opportunity: Facilitates load transfer to fuselage frame

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual and tactile inspection of spar socket, anti-rotation key, and center datum

verification_focus.check: Verify lift, landing, and payload loads trace through spar to fuselage frames
Ensure heat from sun is transferred to cooling airflow over wing surface
Test spar and rib attachment points under simulated load conditions
Confirm battery removal access through wing root service cutout
Validate electrical harness routing from avionics bay to wing tip sensors

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Servo pockets, control horns, pushrod/linkage channels

physics_paths.path_description: Elevon control horn linkages move in response to servo actuation
Cable harnesses and electrical connections provide power and control signals to the servos
Heat generated by servos is dissipated through the foam core and CFRP spar
Access cover allows for maintenance and inspection of the servos

integration_opportunities.opportunity_description: Integration of control horn attachment points for precise linkage alignment
Routing of harnesses through channels to minimize interference with moving parts
Design of thermal dissipation channels to enhance heat removal from servos

verification_focus.focus_description: Ensure the mass of the subsystem does not exceed the budgeted 100g
Verify that all interfaces are correctly aligned and that the assembly order is logical
Test the motion and electrical connectivity of the elevon control system
